exemplar.title: To what extent can Novo Nordisk use Wegovy to further strengthen its market leadership position in the anti-obesity drugs market?

detailedExemplarData.title: To what extent can Novo Nordisk use Wegovy to further strengthen its market leadership position in the anti-obesity drugs market?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Clear definition of change and multiple applications in the adaptation of semaglutide
Good integration of company-specific data on R&D spend and production capacity investments
weaknesses: Key concept of change is not fully woven through every analytical section
Lacks explicit use of change-management theory (e.g., Kotterâs model) to underpin arguments
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates a strong understanding of the key concept âchangeâ with clear analytical thinking and multiple applications to Novo Nordiskâs strategy, matching band 4. However, the concept is not fully integrated in every section and would benefit from deeper theoretical grounding to reach band 5.

strengths: Exactly five high-quality, industry-relevant sources (FT, The Economist, annual report)
Each document is clearly linked to specific analytical frameworks
weaknesses: Depth could be improved by further diversifying perspectives
No critical annotations on source limitations or biases
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work meets the rubric for number of documents and their relevance, placing it at band 3. While documents are well-chosen and linked, the depth of analysis and critical engagement with source limitations prevents a top-band mark.

strengths: Effective selection of key strategic tools (Ansoff, BCG, marketing mix)
Demonstrated initiative in generating a custom Ansoff Matrix for Wegovy
weaknesses: Application often remains descriptive with limited critical evaluation
Missing quantitative precision (e.g., market-share percentages on BCG axes)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Tools are appropriately chosen and relevant, but their application is unevenâsometimes descriptive rather than evaluativeâaligning with band 3 (âadequately appliedâ).
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Use of multiple quantitative data points (market growth, margins, R&D spend) to support arguments
Coherent linkage of pricing analysis to profit margins and funding implications
weaknesses: Occasional data points are presented without explicit strategic linkage
Limited evaluation of alternative strategic options and integration of change theory
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The analysis is largely effective and sustained, using appropriate data and tying it to the research question, meriting band 4. To reach band 5, the student would need to deepen integration of theoretical critique and present a more thorough evaluation of alternatives.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the evidence presented
The research question is explicitly answered in the conclusion
weaknesses: Lacks forward-looking recommendations grounded in change-management theory
Tends to restate analysis rather than offering strategic implications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the conclusions accurately reflect the analysis and directly answer the research question (band 3), they fall short in providing forward-looking, theory-based recommendations, placing them at band 2.

strengths: Logical business-report structure with clear headings and sections
Comprehensive table of contents enhances navigability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The IA exhibits an appropriate and coherent structure throughout, fulfilling all requirements for the top band (band 2).

strengths: All required presentation elements are included (title page, TOC, references, appendices)
Appendices and page numbering are clear and consistent
weaknesses: Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting (italics, URLs)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All presentation requirements are met, meriting 2 marks. Minor formatting inconsistencies in the bibliography do not detract from the completeness of the presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The IA follows a logical business-report structure with title page, TOC, analytical sections, conclusion, bibliography and appendices. Headings and page divisions are clear, so organization is **appropriate throughout**, matching the maximum band for this strand.

All five documents are drawn from **high-quality, industry-relevant sources** (Financial Times, The Economist, Novo Nordisk Annual Report). Each is summarised and linked to specific analytical frameworks, showing depth. The mix of perspectives (supportive and critical) provides a **range of ideas** that directly inform the analysis of production capacity, R&D, pricing and supply issues. Hence the work fits the top-band descriptor of ârelevant, in-depth and providing a range of ideas.â
All **required presentation elements**âtitle page with word count, table of contents, references, and numbered appendicesâare present. Therefore the strand merits the full 2 marks.

Exactly **five supporting documents** are provided and clearly itemised in the overview table, meeting the rubric requirement of 3-5 documents. This places the work in the 3-mark band by default, as bands 2-4 share the same numerical criterion.

Application is generally sound: the student maps Wegovy onto the BCG matrix, links market and product development strategies to Ansoff, and discusses pricing and capacity under the marketing mix. **However, much of the commentary is descriptive** (e.g., restating financial figures) with limited critical depth or comparison of alternatives, so the relevance is clear but the effectiveness is mixed. This aligns with band 3: ârelevance not always clear but adequately applied.â

The student cites **multiple quantitative data points** (market size projections, growth rates, margins, R&D percentages, debt-equity ratios) from both primary and secondary sources. Data are embedded in the narrative and used to justify strategic positions. Selection is ample and appropriate, satisfying the descriptor of âsufficient selection and use,â but occasional figures lack explicit linkage to decisions, preventing a jump to the âeffectiveâ band 5.

Analysis connects data and theory to the research question in a **mostly effective manner**: the student outlines how Wegovyâs star status necessitates investment, evaluates pricing barriers, and weighs R&D spending versus competitors. Integration of change could be deeper and evaluation of alternatives is limited, but overall reasoning is coherent and largely substantiated, fitting the band 4 descriptor of âmostly effective analysis with some integration.â

The research question is **explicitly answered** in the opening sentence of the conclusion (âto a great extentâ) and is supported by a balanced discussion of strengths and threats. This meets the descriptor for band 3: âexplicitly answers research question.â

The IA employs **three well-chosen strategic tools**âAnsoff Matrix, BCG Matrix and elements of the marketing mixâeach appropriate to the research question. Although the repertoire is not extensive, the tools selected are those most directly suited to growth and competitive positioning, so the selection is judged **effective** rather than merely adequate.

The conclusion reiterates key analytical findingsâmarket/product development success, star-to-cash-cow trajectory, pricing and supply risksâand ties them back to the evidence presented. No claims contradict the data, so **consistency with evidence is high**, warranting the top band.

Analysis is **consistently rooted in Novo Nordiskâs real data, strategies and constraints**: sales growth figures, R&D spend, cash-flow metrics, manufacturing investments, and competitive comparisons with Eli Lilly are all used to support arguments.  
Every major tool (Ansoff, BCG, marketing mix) is applied directly to Wegovy and supported by quantitative or qualitative evidence from the company. References to supply bottlenecks, dividend policy and debt ratios further demonstrate integration across the IA.  
Because this company-specific discussion is maintained **throughout the entire assessment**, the strand fulfils the descriptor of âcomprehensive analysis fully integrated,â meriting band 5.

The student defines the key concept of **change** in the introduction and revisits it when explaining how Novo Nordisk adapted semaglutide from a diabetes to an obesity drug and how this strategic shift requires ongoing adaptation of production and R&D.  
â¢ Throughout the analytical sections, however, the discussion of change is intermittent; most paragraphs focus on growth tools (Ansoff, BCG, 7 Ps) rather than explicitly analysing different dimensions of change.  
â¢ Because the concept is applied in several sectionsâbut not thoroughly woven into every stage of the analysisâthe work matches the descriptor of a **âstrong understanding with partially integrated analysisâ** (band 4), rather than the fully-integrated standard required for band 5.

annotations.comment: The research question and key concept are prominently stated, providing clear focus and direction for the assessment.
The definition of âchangeâ is too generic. Contextualize it by linking directly to strategic adaptation processes at Novo Nordisk to deepen conceptual integration.
The table of contents is comprehensive and well-structured, demonstrating strong organizational presentation.
The introduction offers a broad definition of change but does not tie this definition back to Novo Nordiskâs unique strategic environment; integrate with company context.
Effective use of statistical data on obesity prevalence and market projections enhances the credibility of the introduction.
Incorporate established change-management models (e.g., Kotterâs 8 steps) to underpin your recommendations and strengthen conceptual integration.
The bibliography includes all required sections (title, TOC, references), demonstrating full compliance with presentation requirements.
Several bibliography entries lack consistent formatting (e.g., missing italics, uneven URL presentation); standardize to uniform MLA style.
Revise entries for uniform capitalization, italicization of titles, and consistent access-date formatting to enhance professionalism.
When quoting market-growth figures, explicitly connect those numbers to how changing trends drive Novo Nordiskâs strategic adaptation.
The methodological statement âI will analyseâ¦ through Ansoff Matrixâ¦â is descriptive; focus instead on articulating critical insights and evaluation.
Generating your own Ansoff Matrix shows effective tool selection and initiative in applying theory to Novo Nordiskâs context.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The description of Figure 1 would benefit from a deeper critique of how market and product development choices balance risk and reward.
Proposing clinical trials for paediatric obesity shows creative market-extension thinking but requires discussion of regulatory and ethical considerations.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The R&D pool figure is presented descriptively; link how scaling research capacity exemplifies organizational change and competitive advantage.
How might the company navigate paediatric trial ethics and regulatory requirements when extending Wegovy to children?
Classifying Wegovy as a âstarâ product aligns well with BCG principles and highlights strength in tool selection.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The BCG matrix lacks axes labels with actual market-share and growth percentages; add quantitative data for precision.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Incorporate market-share and growth figures into the matrix and discuss how fluctuations would alter Wegovyâs positioning.
The analysis of pricing demonstrates clear linkage to profit margins and R&D funding implications.
The marketing-mix section focuses only on pricing; consider analysing other Ps (e.g., place, promotion) for a comprehensive view.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Analyze distribution strategies under âplaceâ (e.g., partnerships, geographic rollout) to enrich the marketing mix evaluation.
Discussion of pricing as a barrier is insightful but would benefit from evaluating tiered or reimbursement options to address affordability.
The overview of capacity investments in the US and Denmark shows strong integration of company-specific data.
To reinforce the key concept of change, discuss how production-capacity shifts exemplify organisational adaptation processes.
The conclusion explicitly addresses the research question and synthesizes the frameworks used, ensuring consistency with evidence.
While the conclusion restates analysis, it lacks forward-looking recommendations grounded in change-management theory.
